root_text.an5.19:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu neva attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāya.
root_text.an5.19:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu attanā na sīlasampanno hoti, no paraṁ sīlasampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.19:1.4: attanā na samādhisampanno hoti, no paraṁ samādhisampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.19:1.5: attanā na paññāsampanno hoti, no paraṁ paññāsampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.19:1.6: attanā na vimuttisampanno hoti, no paraṁ vimuttisampadāya samādapeti;
root_text.an5.19:1.7: attanā na vimuttiñāṇadassanasampanno hoti, no paraṁ vimuttiñāṇadassanasampadāya samādapeti.
root_text.an5.19:1.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu neva attahitāya paṭipanno hoti, no parahitāyā”ti.

translation_text.an5.19:1.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with five qualities is practicing neither for their own welfare, nor that of others.
translation_text.an5.19:1.3: It’s when a mendicant is not personally accomplished in ethics, immersion, wisdom, freedom, or the knowledge and vision of freedom. Nor do they encourage others in these qualities.
translation_text.an5.19:1.8: A mendicant with these five qualities is practicing neither for their own welfare, nor that of others.”

reference_text.an5.19:1.1: csp1ed16.12, csp2ed16.12, dr22.15, ms16A5_67, msdiv19, ndp17.284, pts-vp-pli3.14, vri37.13
